Brown went 49-587-5 in 2025 and will be a free agent in 2026. He showed that he can still be a contributor and should find his way onto a roster in 2026.

Similar to Tyrone Tracy, Brashard Smith spent most of his collegiate career as a WR. After transferring to SMU from Miami, Smith changed positions to RB and was the central part of their offensive success. Lining up both wide and in the backfield, he is an intriguing weapon with great short-area quickness and ability. We saw flashes of that in Year 1, although the Chiefs appeared hesitant to truly unleash him. Smith will remain no more than a part-timer moving forward, especially after the signing of Kenneth Walker.
Richardson lost a camp competition to Daniel Jones in 2025. Then, when Jones suffered an Achilles tear, Richardson wasn't able to play due to a freak eye injury. He requested a trade in April.

Hurst has two straight seasons of 960+ receiving yards and 6+ TDs after transferring to Georgia State from Valdosta State. He is a tall, rangy WR whose good hands made him a reliable target throughout the past two seasons. Hurst could be developed into the heir to Mike Evans as the big downfield X, but it may take time before he is ready to contribute.

Wright was one of the speediest RBs in the 2024 class. According to The Athletic’s Bruce Feldman, Wright hit a Tyreek Hill-like 23.6 MPH in practice. The three-year Volunteer averaged a ridiculous 7.4 yards per carry in 2023, with 19 of his 136 attempts going for 15+ yards (per PFF). He showed out at the Combine with a 4.38 forty time and a 38-inch vertical. Miami provided an ideal landing spot for creative usage, but 2024 turned up light work behind De'Von Achane. The same trend surfaced in '25, while Miami also added rookie RB Ollie Gordon. We'll see how this backfield depth shakes out under a new coaching staff.
Mayer flashed his talent on a poor Raiders offense when Brock Bowers was injured. There's just not projectable fantasy value here, at least for 2026. Mayer is slated for free agency in 2027.

Blue was expected to be the next big Texas RB after Sophomore CJ Baxter went down with an injury. However, struggles with his vision early in 2024 led to a pass-catching role for the former four-star recruit. Blue ran a 4.38 at the NFL Combine despite nursing a torn muscle in his groin. His 4.25 Pro Day time may be more accurate to his actual speed. As the first RB drafted by the Cowboys, Blue carried some appeal entering the '25 season. But he barely saw the field and watched as Javonte Williams rebounded in a major way. Dallas then re-signed Williams to a three-year contract, putting a lid on Blue's dynasty value.

Tillman dealt with multiple injuries in 2025, only playing in 13 games. His 21-270-2 stat line was a step back from 2024, but the Browns horrible passing offense may be to blame. A new offensive system and QB could benefit Tillman in 2026.

Smith was a major fantasy flop in 2025, posting only two top-12 fantasy finishes. At 35, his days as an NFL starter are nearing an end. But he'll at least have a shot at starting games in 2026 after the Jets acquired him in a trade from Vegas.

Washington got a shot to play when Tyreek Hill went down with an injury. He went 46-317-3 in 2025, earning the slot role. With a new head coach next year, Washington's role is unknown, but he is worth keeping on the roster to see how the offseason goes.

Austin worked as the No. 2 WR behind D.K. Metcalf in 2025. He flashed when Metcalf was out due to suspension. He is unlikely to be a long term answer, but he should stick on the Steelers with a role in 2026 and beyond.

Davis' college tape featured a nice combo of power, patience, and vision. The Jets' landing spot just hasn't been a friendly one, especially with Breece Hall returning on the franchise tag. Davis will also be pushed by third-year RB Braelon Allen.

Brother of Trey Lance, Bryce posted two straight 1,000-yard seasons and even caught 17 TDs in 2024. At 6'3, 210 pounds, Lance has the size and leaping ability to dominate at the FCS level. He certainly carries his brother's athleticism, but the level of competition brings major questions. Lance lasted until Day 3, but the Saints are a good spot for him to make the roster. He could ascend to the No. 3 WR behind Chris Olave and Jordyn Tyson.

Allen had his background looked into deeply by the NFL after having an assault charge dismissed and expunged from his record. With that aside, he exited college with two straight 1,000-yard rushing seasons and 102 receptions over that period. His multi-faceted skill set makes him a dynasty sleeper, even after a quiet rookie season.

Allen returned to the Chargers and had a strong early season. He ended up going 81-777-4 in 2025, but will turn 34 in the offseason. Allen may have one year left in his NFL career, but will likely have to take a back-up role. It is unlikely he sticks as a fantasy starter in 2026, and certainly is nearing retirement.

Brazzell struggled in 2024, but the 6'5 WR emerged as the top threat on Tennessee in 2025. He went 62-1017-9, often working downfield. His rare blend of height and speed gives Brazzell plenty of upside, but he lacks the nuanced route running and can short-arm catches. Brazzell will likely end up as a downfield threat for the Panthers who could develop into a starter.

Thornton had plenty of preseason hype, but that didn't come to fruition in his rookie year. Thornton went 10-135-0 during the 2025 season despite often playing an outside role early in the season. Perhaps a new coaching staff can maximize Thornton's size and speed.

Bigsby wrapped up a productive career at Auburn with 540-2,903-25 (35 games). He tested as an average athlete at the Combine, however, and his struggles carried over into a mistake-filled first season. Bigsby looked like a different back in 2024, showing the power and burst that made him an attractive prospect. A wrench was thrown into his 2025 plans, as the Jags traded him to Philly over the summer. He’ll remain in a backup/handcuff role.
Down-to-down inconsistency remained after signing with the Jets, as Fields was eventually benched for Tyrod Taylor. Fields will likely find a backup job in 2026.

Lane took a back seat to teammate Makai Lemon, but don't overlook the production here. He went 49-745-4 in 2025, following up a 12 TD season in 2024. The 6'4, 195-pound WR explodes off the line and uses his length to beat the press. Lane got Day 2 draft capital with a new Ravens coaching staff. They also drafted Elijah Sarratt though. There is an opening for Lane to emerge as a starter for the Ravens.

Sarratt flashed in 2024 going 53-957-8. He missed a few games due to a hamstring injury, but he did score 13 TDs in 2025 with Fernando Mendoza at QB. Sarratt has high-end size at 6'2, 213 pounds. His high pointing is among the best in the class. The Ravens drafted WR Ja'Kobi Lane over Sarratt, but they will likely compete for targets as rookies.

Mooney took a step back in 2025 going 32-443-1 following up his nearly 1,000 yard season in 2024. Mooney battled injuries throughout the season, but he clearly lost a step in his age-28 season. The Falcons may address the WR position this offseason pushing Mooney further down the depth chart.

Benson entered the league with plenty of tread on his tires after tallying 156 and 154 carries over his final two college seasons, but a healthy James Conner limited the rookie’s impact. Then in 2025, Benson missed all but 4 games with a knee injury. He won't be guaranteed a roster spot in 2026 under a new coaching staff. Arizona added top-five pick Jeremiyah Love and veteran Tyler Allgeier in free agency.

Klubnik came into college football as a five-star recruit who became a starter before the end of his first season. He never reached his potential, especially in terms of developing more arm strength. He flashed some athleticism in 2024, going for 119-463-7 on the ground. He could be a late-round draft pick with some upside.

Bell transferred to UConn in 2024 and broke out. Over the past two seasons, he caught 151-2138-18. He isn't built like an NFL WR, but the production is hard to ignore with Bell able to beat man coverage. He lands in a great situation with Josh Allen as a potential No. 3 WR on the Bills.

Thompson played at Oklahoma and Texas early in his career, but barely saw the field. He made the jump to Mississippi State in 2025 and produced, going 57-1054-6 on the season. Thompson is a big play threat who could challenge to be the fastest WR at the NFL Combine this year. Thompson is pretty blocked on the Chargers depth chart, but he may provide an occasional deep threat.

Davis tallied back-to-back 1,000-yard rushing seasons -- one at Vanderbilt and one at Kentucky (2023). 2023 turned up his best work yet, including 3.81 yards after contact per attempt and a career-long 75-yarder on a sizable 73% carry share. After a promising rookie year, Davis' workload regressed in 2025, with 34% of his touches coming in a meaningless Week 18 game. He'll remain far behind James Cook in 2026.

Randall came to Clemson as a WR, but transitioned to RB before 2025. The 6'2, 235 back was a track star in high school, so his size and speed offers a unique runner who succeeded on a weak Clemson offense. Randall could fill the Keaton Mitchell role down the line as a change of pace receiving back.

As a rookie, Tucker watched Rachaad White eventually earn a workhorse role. Then in 2024, he witnessed the breakout of Bucky Irving. Outside of one game vs. Buffalo, Tucker remained quiet in 2025. He returned to Tampa Bay in March, but the signing of Kenneth Gainwell ruins any shot at '26 value.
Gabriel was a surprise third-round pick for the Browns in 2025. He eventually saw the field but didn't show anything to get excited about for fantasy.

Vidal is short but stout at 5’8, 215 pounds. He posted eye-popping production at Troy that includes 2,793 rushing yards and 24 TDs over his final two seasons. After a quiet 2024, injuries to Najee Harris and Omarion Hampton opened the door for Vidal to post five games of 100+ yards. He'll slide back to a reserve role in '26.

Milroe exited college with one of the best QB rushing profiles since Lamar Jackson. He'll be developed as a passer behind Sam Darnold, who excelled as the 2025 starter. If this staff can figure out how to harness Milroe’s passing, he could be gangbusters for fantasy in a few years.

Vele was traded from the Broncos to the Saints at the trade deadline. He flashed with an 8-93-1 game with New Orleans, but his usage wasn't consistent enough to be fantasy relevant. Vele should be rostered in deeper dynasty leagues in case he wins a starting spot with QB Tyler Shough growing.

Guerendo brings excellent long speed, and he showed it at the Combine with a 4.33 forty-time. He also jumped 41.5 inches and tallied a 10’9” broad jump – all at 221 pounds. We saw that in a narrow window in 2024, although injuries limited his late-season impact. The 49ers showed their lack of faith in Guerendo by using a Round 5 pick on '25 rookie Jordan James. They also traded for Brian Robinson Jr. Ultimately, Guerendo is just a dynasty stash.

Black split the backfield at Indiana in 2025 with Roman Hemby, but did still go 186-1040-10 with 5.5 yards per carry. Despite not being invited to the NFL Combine, he was the third RB selected in the 2026 NFL Draft. Black will get the opportunity to compete for the backup RB job to Christian McCaffrey, but still lacks fantasy upside to become a difference maker.

Johnson's rookie year proved disastrous. After arriving in Round 3, the Iowa standout spread only 28 carries (for 69 yards) across 10 games. He simply wasn't able to earn a role alongside Jaylen Warren and Kenneth Gainwell. We then saw how little the team believes in Johnson, as Rico Dowdle arrived on a two-year deal. Note that Warren is signed through the 2027 season.

Legette had a disappointing sophomore season going 35-363-3. He was beaten out by rookie Tetairoa McMillan and played more of a reserve role this season. With the Panthers' struggles in the passing game, there is little upside for Legette moving forward, except for him only being 25 years old.

Wilson saw a surprising 103 carries in 2024, posting 4.9 YPC. His workload rose in '25, largely because of missed games from Josh Jacobs. Wilson hit the spring as a free agent and signed with a Seahawks team that just lost Kenneth Walker to the Chiefs. Zach Charbonnet is also coming off a January ACL tear. Then came the NFL Draft, when serious competition arrived in Round 1 pick Jadarian Price. The rookie puts a major dent into Wilson's dynasty value.

James is a young and explosive runner who is an ideal zone scheme fit. He posted 1,267 rushing yards in his final Oregon season in a split backfield and then went in Round 5 of the 2025 draft. He didn't see a single touch as a rookie, though. There's only an outside chance he's San Francisco's RB2 in 2026.

Fields excelled at Virginia with back-to-back 800-yard receiving seasons. He transferred to Notre Dame in 2025, and while he continued to showcase his size and contested ability, the production ultimately dropped. He's also on the older side, hitting 24 before the start of the '26 season. There certainly is an opening for Fields to start for the Giants, but he is unlikely to be a consistent fantasy threat.

Hunter made a name for himself dominating Alabama as a sophomore, but saved his best season for his senior year in '24. With 1,201 yards rushing and another 155 through the air, he is an intriguing two-way player with a strong lower body to work in traffic. However, he didn't see the field in 2025, as Kyren Williams and Blake Corum handled almost all of the backfield work. That duo remains under contract for 2026.

Claiborne injured his ribs in the first game of the year that seemed to affect him throughout the season. He is a smaller runner with 4.3 speed who can break long runs. He has 21 rushing TDs over the last two years on a weak Wake Forest offense. Claiborne could certainly make the Vikings roster as the third RB and make an impact in 2027.

Douglas is a reliable possession WR at 6'4, 205 pounds. He went 54-846-7 last year at Texas Tech as a reliable option in the medium to deep range. His big extension allows for contested catches despite Douglas not having the athleticism to get much separation. Douglas was overdrafted on Day 2, but the WR depth chart is wide open to make an impact on the Dolphins.
Jones finished as a top-15 fantasy QB in five of eight starts in 2025. He's under contract for one more season, and the 49ers are expected to retain him as the backup.

Johnson stepped up when injuries ravaged the Buccaneers' WRs in 2025. He finished his rookie season going 28-322-5. The depth chart may not give Johnson an opportunity to play a lot moving forward, but his talent makes him rosterable in most dynasty league formats.

Ridley had a disappointing 2025 going 17-303-0. He will likely move on to a new team, but he recently turned 31. Ridley's best days are behind him, but he could be useful in fantasy if he lands in a pass heavy system with a good QB.

An AC Joint sprain to CeeDee Lamb caused Flournoy to get some playing time and take advnatage. He ended 2025 going 40-475-5, but flashed great hands and route running. He should stick on the Cowboys roster and could be among the top three WRs on the team in 2026 and beyond.

Thomas showed promise back in 2023 at Mississippi State before transferring up to LSU. He went 41-488-4, showcasing good deep speed and the ability to beat the press off the snap. Thomas was drafted on Day 2 by the Bears, but he profiles more as a returner. He could also play in the slot if Kalif Raymond gets hurt.

Douglas had the worst season of his NFL career in 2025. He went 31-447-3, the lowest reception total of his career. Despite the lack of Patriots' WR options, Douglas couldn't really break through in Drake Maye's second season. He should stick around and have a role, but there is little upside to Douglas going forward.
In 2025, Shipley played a minor role behind Saquon Barkley, who didn't miss a game due to injury. Barkley and Tank Bigsby remain under contract for '26, limiting Shipley's dynasty appeal.
Lightly used on the ground in 2024, Hill still managed to set a career-high with 42 catches. Injuries limited Hill to only 18 carries and 21 catches in 2025. He remains a low-upside dynasty asset.

Bond went undrafted in April's draft but was signed by the Browns when cleared of off field charges. He was on the field often, ending the season going 18-338-0. A high ankle sprain cut his season short, but Bond showed enough to stick on the Browns roster as a young, talented WR.

Law was a former top recruit for Alabama but broke out in 2025 at Kentucky. He went 53-540-3. A player who can play in the slot or outside, Law has good athleticism and soft hands. Law will battle to make the Lions roster.
Durability remained an issue here in 2025. Miller's now totaled only 127 carries through three seasons. 2026 turns up a contract year.

Green spent two years at Boise State before transferring to Arkansas. He has a strong arm, but Green's advantage is in his rushing ability. Over the past two years, he has rushed for a combined 1,379 yards and 16 TDs. Standing at 6'6 and 235 pounds, Green is an athletic freak who the Browns may bring along to develop in a weak QB room.

Mims Jr. fell behind Troy Franklin and Pat Bryan in the WR pecking order. He went 37-322-1, but Mims is still an elite returner who will stick on the roster. He just won't have the fantasy impact that was promised when he was drafted.

Lloyd’s college career began at South Carolina with a 2020 ACL tear. A quiet two-year stretch followed – then a move to USC. In 2023, he handled a 1A role alongside fifth-year senior Austin Jones (116 carries to 85). Lloyd displayed plus vision, acceleration, and open-field elusiveness en route to 7.1 yards per carry. Injuries ruined his 2024 and 2025 seasons, but there's some deep sleeper appeal here if Lloyd can swat the injury bug.

Etienne showed pro potential at Florida, but he played behind a true freshman after transferring to Georgia. Like his brother Travis, Trevor has pass-catching ability that could eventually be utilized in the pros. However, he may be more of a third-down back than a starter. His rookie year turned up only 23 touches behind Rico Dowdle and Chuba Hubbard.

Engram had high hopes for 2025, but he fell short of expectations (50-461-1). He now heads into his age-32 season in an offense that's suddenly stacked at WR. We're not banking on a 2026 rebound here.
Nailor continued flashing in 2025 especially in the red zone. He could be a starter for the Raiders with their lack of WR depth

In March of 2024, Singletary inked a three-year, $16.5 million deal with New York. But a poor offensive environment and the rise of Tyrone Tracy Jr. kept Singletary's fantasy production in check that season. The Giants added Cam Skattebo in the '25 draft, although his season-ending injury allowed Singletary to enter a timeshare with Tracy. With Skattebo coming back, Singletary won't be a lock for the 2026 roster.

Payton is a small school prospect with only one year of starting. He led a successful NDSU offense throwing for 2,719 yards with 16 TDs and 4 INTs. He will be a developmental back-up on the Eagles, but his athletic upside could give him fantasy potential if Jalen Hurts goes down.

Bateman continues struggling with the Ravens. He only went 19-224-2 in 2025. He signed a new deal with the Ravens and returns for the forseeable future, but a new head coach could mean Bateman loses his starting role.
Palmer signed a sizable deal with the Bills, but he only went 22-303-0 in 2025. Palmer's role is unlikely to grow in the future as the Bills should add more WRs in the offseason.
The Browns showed their cards on Ford after adding Quinshon Judkins and Dylan Sampson in the 2025 draft. He joined Washington on a one-year deal in March, but his roster spot isn't guaranteed.
Kupp is nearing the end of his career as he approaches his age-33 season. He took a backseat to JSN going 47-593-2 in 2025. Kupp may have a year or two left, but he won't be startable in fantasy.

Virgil transferred from Miami (OH) to Texas Tech, showcasing his speed as a threat next to WR Caleb Douglas. Virgil is 6'3 with a big wingspan and offered a downfield contested catch option. Virgil went early in Round 5 and could crack the Cardinals depth chart down the line as a No. 4 WR.

McGowan started at Oklahoma but was charged with robbery and battery on a drug-related crime. He transferred to New Mexico State and succeeded as their starter. Finally, he ended in Kentucky this past year, rushing for 165-725-12. McGowan fell to the seventh round, but the depth chart is clear for him to make the roster and compete to back-up Jonathan Taylor

Kirk started the season as the slot WR for the Texans, but a hamstring ended his 2025 season. He will be a free agent this offseason and could sign somewhere that gives him a role. But Kirk's best days are behind him.

Sanders ended as TE47 in PPR points per game in 2025, a disappointing season. A poor Panthers' passing game hurt Sanders' output, but at only 23, there's still a chance of him developing into a useful fantasy piece. Carolina remains thin at TE ahead of the NFL Draft.

Coleman was a four-star recruit who started with Deion Sanders at Jackson State. Instead of following Sanders to Colorado, Coleman went to Louisville, but struggled to make an impact. Then he went to Missouri. 2024 was Coleman's best season at Missouri going 74-932-6. Coleman will likely back-up Malik Washington as the slot WR for the Dolphins.
Lane mostly played on special teams for the Commanders, but his speed was utilized on offense at times. He has some upside if he develops, but Lane likely sits toward the end of the depth chart.
Harris missed almost all of the 2025 season with a torn Achilles. He hits free agency as a long-shot to find a lead-back role.

Taylor saw playing time in his rookie season as the de facto starter for the Jets, although he only mustered 44-369-1. Neck and ankle injuries caused miss games during the season, but his playing time stayed high. While Geno Smith arrived to improve the QB spot, this passing game still profiles as a bottom-five unit.

Cousins returned to the starting lineup following Michael Penix's ACL tear last season. The results were mixed, though, and Cousins will play in Vegas in 2026. He's expected to open 2026 as the starter while the team brings along top pick Fernando Mendoza.

Benson is a speed demon who never quite broke out after his JUCO stint. After flopping at Alabama, Benson took the Evan Stewart deep role at Oregon this past season. Benson offers another deep threat for the Raiders behind Tre Tucker and Dont'e Thornton.

Royals did very little in his rookie year even with Rashee Rice missing most of the season. He only caught two passes in 2025, but could develop into a bigger role in 2026 if other WRs move on from the Chiefs. He is worth hanging onto in case there is offseason buzz.

Giddens had two straight 1,000-yard rushing seasons for Kansas State (2023-2024), showing power and quickness as the lead back. He may not quite have the full range of NFL athleticism, but Giddens could eventually develop into a role player. He managed only 26 carries (3.7 YPC) as a rookie.
Watson sustained an Achilles tear in Week 7 of the 2024 season. There’s a chance he doesn’t start another NFL game, although he remains under contract with the Browns.

Heidenreich played the slot back in Navy's option offense, playing in the slot but also taking part in the triple option. That led to going 77-499-3 as a runner and 51-941-6 as a receiver in 2025. He even threw a pass this season. Heidenreich's role on the Steelers remains to be seen, but he may struggle to crack the 53-man roster.

Two straight 1,500-yard rushing seasons with double-digit TDs show Brooks’ success at Texas Tech. His shifty feet lead to very few hard hits, and Brooks can certainly hit the home run. Outside of missing one game in 2024 with an arm injury, Brooks has been healthy despite being a bellcow with 576 carries over the past two seasons. He didn't play much as a rookie but could compete for the RB2 role in 2026.

Williams flashed a bit as a rookie, but he remained a unique weapon used in the screen game and trick plays. Williams will have trouble cracking a deep Packers' WR group, but he could find more playing time in 2026.

Miller never had a breakout season for Alabama, topping out at 504 rushing yards in 2025. His lack of production is concerning, but Miller's skill set as a stout RB who moves nimbly in short spaces makes him an attractive depth RB in the NFL. Miller could make the Patriots roster as a third RB and get some carries if one of the top RBs go down.

Mixon quietly assembled the PPR RB6 season in 2023. After a hot start in 2024, his play declined down the stretch. Then in 2025, he missed the entire season with a mysterious foot injury. He'll play elsewhere in 2026.

Gesicki returned as the Bengals' starting TE in 2025, but an injury to Joe Burrow and a step back for the offense made him unstartable for most of the year. With the bevy of weapons on the Bengals, Gesicki will remain a spot-starter in the short term.
Felton was mostly pegged as a special teams and developmental player for the Vikings. He didn't make much impact as a rookie, but should stick on the roster for the future and could have some upside.
McLaughlin posted massive numbers across a five-year college career (1,250-8,166-79, plus 66 catches). But his value is trending down after registering only 37 carries (in 8 games) this past season.

Iosivas continues working as the No. 3 WR for the Bengals behind Ja'Marr Chase and Tee Higgins. He saw a boost when one of those players were out and can be useful in deeper leagues. Iosivas' athleticism should allow him to stick in the league for awhile.

Rodgers exceeded expectations in 2025 with 3,322 yards, 24 TDs, and 7 INTs. Still, it's unknown whether he'll return for one more season in 2026. If so, it'll almost certainly be with Pittsburgh and new HC Mike McCarthy.

Henderson was a former five-star RB recruit who went to Alabama and converted to WR. After little playing time for three years, he transferred to Kansas and broke out with 45-766-5. Possessing great speed and explosion, Henderson looks the part, although he is still very raw in terms of routes and technique. He enters an open Seahawks WR depth chart where Henderson can make the team with upside.

Delp is an H-Back who continues in the line of great Georgia TEs. He went 20-261-1 and was used as a lead blocker often throughout the season. That blocking may get him drafted higher than his production indicates, but Delp's soft hands could be enough to become a better pro than college player. Delp could develop into the starting TE for the Saints. The team has a potential out for Juwan Johnson after this year.

Caldwell transferred to Cincinnati from Lindenwood and flashed incredible athleticism in 2025. He's 6'4, 210 pounds and will be a Combine stud both in the vertical and 40 time. He will have to battle to make the Chiefs after going undrafted.

Klare broke out at Purdue in 2024 with 51-685-4. He took a step back on the Buckeyes last year at 43-448-2, but he showed improved blocking and good route running to be a shallow target. He did have some drops and mental lapses, but Klare can be a Jake Ferguson-type TE. He will split time with Terrance Ferguson, but the rest of the Rams TEs are set to become FAs within the next two years.

Cameron has had two straight years of 750+ receiving yards and 9+ TDs. The focal point of the Baylor offense, Cameron has good size and physicality down the field at 218 pounds. He's tough to bring down after the catch as well. Cameron is limited athletically, but he has been productive. He will be a reserve on the Jaguars.
Lambert-Smith had preseason hype coming into his rookie season, but it didn't really pay off. He went 5-51-1 as a rookie, but should land on the team next year and may develop more.
Gibson earned a three-year deal in 2024 with $5.3 million guaranteed. Following a quiet 2024, he sustained a torn ACL in Week 5 of 2025. He'll be questionable for Week 1 of the '26 season.

Mafah had two straight 1,000+ total yard seasons at Clemson. A powerful inside runner, Mafah takes good angles to maximize yardage. We just didn't see his traits surface in Year 1, as Javonte Williams enjoyed a rebound season and Malik Davis earned the RB2 role. There's no reason to expect a major jump in production from Mafah in '26.

Burks flashed speed and athleticism at Purdue before transferring to Oklahoma, but he was never able to break out. He went 57-620-4 in 2025 after an injury-riddled 2024. Burks sits as a pure slot WR at 5'9, 188 pounds. Burks had a drastic NFL Draft fall to the 7th round and is unlikely to make a major fantasy impact.

Daniels started at Liberty before transferring to LSU and finally Miami. He was a central cog in the Hurricane's passing game this year to help support QB Carson Beck. Daniels will likely be blocked from moving up the Rams depth chart.

Allen bounced around in college, from Louisiana Tech to Texas A&M to Cincinnati. He led the Bearcats in receiving this past season, going 51-674-13. A shifty slot WR, Allen can create a mismatch with slow-to-react nickel CBs. Allen offers little ceiling, but he will compete with second-year WR Jalen Royals to make the roster.
Carter took over the lead back role after Trey Benson went out with injury, but there isn't much long-term upside here. He likely goes back to being a reserve in 2026 and beyond.
Estime lasted only one season in Denver after arriving as a Round 5 pick. He saw fill-in work with the Saints in 2025 but remains a replacement-level talent.

Brown's best season was his freshman year at Kentucky in 2022. He only had one receiving TD this year for LSU, but his speed should make Brown an all around offensive weapon at the NFL level. Brown likely sticks as a returner, but he could offer a few deep targets for the Saints every now and then.

Chubb struggled to produce leading up to Week 15 of 2024. Then came a broken foot, ending his season. 2025 wasn't much better, as he was outplayed by rookie Woody Marks. At 30, Chubb's very likely done as an impact fantasy player.
Mumpfield saw more snaps late in the season and flashed enough ability to potentially make the Rams in 2026. He is intriguing in very deep leagues on the back end of your dynasty roster.

Thornton found a career resurgence with 19-438-3 on 37 targets, benefiting from injuries to the Chiefs' WR corps. He returned on a one-year deal this offseason and should continue in a downfield/rotational role.

Joly has been a consistent producer, with three straight seasons over 480 yards receiving. He continued thriving after transferring up from UConn to NC State. Joly fell to Day 3, but he has the ability to play HC Sean Payton's "Joker" role on the team after Evan Engram leaves. He has intriguing receiving upside.
In 2025, Demercado posted nice efficiency numbers as a fill-in for injured RBs Trey Benson and James Conner. Still, Demercado projects as no better than the RB3 under new OC Mike LaFleur.

Atwell managed only 6 catches in 10 games for the Rams in 2025. He then signed a one-year deal with a Miami squad that's paper-thin at WR. Still, Atwell's career trajectory -- plus Miami's uncertainty at QB -- makes it hard to forecast a fantasy-friendly 2026.
The Ravens used a 2024 Round 5 pick on Ali, who posted two years of major production at Marshall. He'll continue pushing for scraps left behind by Derrick Henry.

Whittington got some play in 2025, but he was still pretty buried on the depth chart with Davante Adams and the TEs emerging. He could have a long NFL career, but it is doubtful Whittington does enough to make a fantasy impact.

Despite playing with Pat Freiermuth and Jonnu Smith, Washington emerged as a versatile weapon with his size and after-catch ability. Washington later broke his arm and ended up on IR, but he'll return in 2026 and could earn more work for new HC Mike McCarthy.

In 2025, Mariota supplied spot-start value as a fill-in for Jayden Daniels. The veteran notched three top-10 fantasy finishes, peaking at QB3. He signed a new deal to remain Jayden Daniels' backup in 2026.
Horn played mostly on special teams, but he flashed a bit as a receiver. He could make the roster in 2026 and have a bigger impact, but should only be rostered in deep leagues.
Watkins dealt with a high ankle sprain in 2026 and would have had some opportunity to crack the 49ers lineup. He is in a crowded situation going forward.
Johnson recorded only 2 carries in Year 1 under HC Ben Johnson. He certainly isn't a lock to return to the Bears.
Burks flashed in a late-season sample with the Commanders. He hit free agency in the spring but re-signed to compete for snaps in a wide-open depth chart.

Young was the deep threat for Georgia this year with 26-358-1. He was second on the team in receiving yards behind Zachariah Branch and was built to take the top off of the defense. He could become the No. 4 WR for the Bengals.
Perine returned to the Bengals in 2025 and saw his highest carry total (84) since 2022. He'll play next season at 31 and will remain no more than a handcuff.

Howard was generally a mediocre QB in terms of accuracy and arm strength for most of his college career, but he had an outstanding playoff run, throwing for 1,150 yards and 8 TDs (2 INTs). Howard landed in a decent situation with the Steelers to be a potential starter, depending on the future of Aaron Rodgers.

Slayton had a few fantasy-relevant games in 2025, ending the season with 37-538-1. While the Giants lost WR Wan'Dale Robinson, they added WR Darnell Mooney and TE Isaiah Likely to join WR1 Malik Nabers. Slayton projects as a rotational piece.

Raridon stands to be a two-way TE at the next level with solid blocking skills showing development as a pass catcher as well. Hunter Henry is getting older and Raridon's Day 2 draft capital could make him the heir apparent. He offers intriguing upside if you can stash him for a bit.
Johnson enjoyed a solid 2024 with a career-high 3 receiving TDs. He remained a part-timer behind James Cook in 2025 and enters '26 with one year left on his contract.
Kaliakmanis was a surprise pick by the Washington Commanders. He has a good arm, but his lack of accuracy and college success makes him a hard pass in dynasty.
NWI impressed as a deep threat, scoring long TDs in 2024 when the Titans offense. Signing with Miami didn't do him any favors. He only had 11 catches and appears done producing for his career.

Endries transferred from Cal to Texas to replace Gunnar Helm and had a connection with QB Arch Manning. He went 33-346-3. He is a good route runner who can play in-line or in the slot. Endries will have a battle to even make the Bengals roster, but his intriguing receiving upside makes him a stash in very deep dynasty leagues.

Whittington transferred from Western Kentucky to Oregon in 2022 and slowly grew his role on the Ducks. He won the starting job in 2026 despite stiff competition, showcasing short area quickness and receiving ability. Whittington went undrafted but landed on the Texans.

Rocket Sanders looked like a future NFL stud after his sophomore season at Arkansas, rushing for 1,443 yards. But he dealt with multiple injuries and settled in for a mediocre year at South Carolina in 2024. Being nearly 230 pounds with insane explosion off the snap, he does boast some enticing traits. There's just not a clear path to him becoming a valuable fantasy asset.

Moss was a four-star recruit who never quite hit his potential at Texas A&M. He averaged 6.32 yards per carry in 2024, but fell off to 5.25 in 2025. He has never led the team in carries, but has flashed the ability to sidestep defenders on the second level. Moss went undrafted.
Jordan arrived in Round 6 of the '24 draft, but he didn't see action until late in the '25 season. He projects as a reserve going forward.
Smith had good draft capital, but he could barely crack the lineup. He played behind Adonai Mitchell and John Metchie. That doesn't bode well for Smith's future.
The signing of George Pickens killed Tolbert as a productive WR. He will likely be on the roster bubble in 2026.
Hutchinson had a decent snap share despite the Texans drafting two rookies. But, he clearly isn't talented enough to be a consistent fantasy presence. He likely will be on the roster bubble in 2026.

Wetjen is one of the top returners in the draft, but that may translate into draft capital and snaps (as we saw with Chimere Dike last year). He was fifth on Iowa in receiving yards last year (151). He will be a return only player on the Steelers.

Bond has led Boston College in receiving every season since Zay Flowers left for the NFL. He also had 30+ more receptions every season than the player who ranked second on the team. Bond is a bit thin, but he is shifty and has after-the-catch ability. Bond will likely play special teams for the Texans, but may develop into a WR who gets some snaps.

McCaffrey got some work in 2025 when injuries hit the Commanders' WRs, but a broken collarbone ended his season. McCaffrey should stick on the roster, but he will likely have to compete with incoming rookies/FAs for a contributing spot.

A developmental QB, Leonard saw some action in 2025 after injuries hit the QB room. He flashed with two rushing TDs and a surprising 270-2 passing line against Houston in Week 18. Still, we're likely looking at a long-term backup here.
McKee brings enticing physical tools, but he’s stuck behind Jalen Hurts as Philly’s QB2. At 26 next season, there's just an outside shot that McKee ever becomes a starter.

Turpin makes his money on special teams, but the Cowboys have used him here and there for gadget plays/designed touches. Barring injuries ahead of him, there's no fantasy value outside of the return game.

Smith endured a disappointing season after being traded to the Steelers. He ended as TE45 in PPR points per game and was more effective running the ball than catching. He hits free agency ahead of his age-31 season.

Henry went 151-1045-9 on the ground for UTSA this year. He was dominant to start the year with three straight games of 140+ rushing yards and 2+ TDs. An ankle injury limited Henry late in the year, but he flashed NFL talent. Henry went undrafted.

Altmyer found success as a three-year starter for the Illini. He peaked in 2025, throwing for 3,007 yards and 22 TDs. He also ran for over 200 yards and 3 TDs every season. He wasn't drafted and will have an uphill battle to make the Lions
Badie never became a factor for the Broncos in 2025, even after the season-ending loss of J.K. Dobbins. Badie now enters his fifth season with only 20 career carries.

Rivers led Georgia Tech in receiving in 2025, going 46-658-2. He's also a useful punt returner. Rivers runs smooth routes and gains separation as a WR, but his lack of size and physical tools. He will have an upward battle to make the Bucs after going undrafted.
Williams was a major reach by the Jaguars and enters a crowded WR depth chart. He went 59-749-6 at Stanford, but doesn't showcase NFL athleticism.

Ott burst onto the scene as a true freshman at Cal in 2022, going 170-897-8 on the ground and 46-321-3 through the air. He followed that up with 1,315 yards and 12 TDs rushing in 2023. Injuries cut his 2024 short, but Ott transferred to Oklahoma in hopes of hitting it big. He never climbed the depth chart, rushing for only 68 yards. Ott went undrafted.

King played for three years at Texas A&M before transferring to Georgia Tech as their three-year starter. He's dealt with injuries throughout his career, including a fractured leg and ankle in 2021 and a torn labrum in 2024. King is only an average thrower, but his rushing ability is the selling point. King went undrafted.

Klein replaced Colston Loveland at Michigan but dealt with nagging injuries over the course of the season. He is another elite athlete who showcases good blocking and soft hands. Klein will need development, but his Day 2 draft capital could make him the future starter at TE for the Texans. He is worth stashing long term if you have space.

Pavia became a Heisman finalist after leading Vanderbilt to a top-25 season. He started his playing career at the New Mexico Military Institute, followed by two years at New Mexico State. He ended his career with two years at Vanderbilt, helping to elevate their offense. Pavia went undrafted.

Roush has improved every season, topping out at 49-545-2 in 2025 for Stanford. At 6'5, 260 pounds, he brings excellent size and blocking ability, but there's also some red zone upside here. Roush will be the No. 2 TE on the Bears, eventually. His Day 2 draft capital means the Bears will likely stick to primarily 12 personnel moving forward.

Restrepo had some UDFA hype after getting paired with his college QB Cam Ward. Restrepo, though, played in two games as a rookie. He had three catches and will compete to even make the Titans' roster in 2026.

Reid dealt with ankle issues in 2025 leading to a poor season. But he was one of the top RBs in 2024 going 52-579-4 through the air and 184-966-5 on the ground. He is small, sitting at 5'8 and 175 pounds, but Reid makes up for it with explosiveness and short area quickness. Reid went undrafted.

Taylor started as a walk-on at North Carolina Central in 2020. He worked his way up to being Virginia's starting RB in 2025, rushing for 222-1062-14. Taylor isn't the most athletic back, but he plays with a low center of gravity and avoids big hits. He is an intriguing NFL backup type. Taylor went undrafted.
Kmet started strong in 2025, but he fell behind rookie Colston Loveland by the end of the season. Even with D.J. Moore in Buffalo, Kmet's targets figure to remain limited going forward.

Daniels flashed early in his career at Kansas, but he never crossed into NFL potential territory. He did throw for 2,531 yards with 22 TDs and 7 INTs in 2025. The turnovers and poor decision-making certainly hurt Daniels' pro prospects. His rushing makes him intriguing, though, with two straight 400+ yard rushing seasons. Daniels went undrafted

Boerkircher is one of the best blocking TEs in the draft, but he boasts an excellent catch radius and range in the red zone. After spending four years as a back-up at Nebraska, he became the top TE for Texas A&M in 2025. Despite only having 19 catches, he did score 3 TDs. Boerkircher's Round 2 draft capital was surprising, but he does everything pretty well (and nothing great). He likely sticks as the Jaguars No. 2 TE, even if Brenton Strange enters free agency after this season.

McAlister had a great season at TCU going 72-1190-10. A big bodied X WR, McAlister was used in a variety of ways on the Horned Frogs. He may not have NFL athleticism, but his hands and physicality at the line could earn him a spot on a roster. He went undrafted.

Trayanum has encountered a volatile college career. A four-star RB recruit at Arizona State, he flashed elite athleticism at 220 pounds as a freshman. He eventually moved to LB and transferred to Ohio State, where he would pivot back to RB. He went 182-1015-12 on the ground this past year, albeit with underwhelming advanced metrics. Trayanum's age and late breakout likely signal a later-round pick.

Koziol went 94-839-8 in 2024 with Ball State before transferring to Houston. His 74-727-6 showcases Koziol's pass-catching prowess. His massive wingspan makes him a mismatch at 6'7, 250 pounds. Koziol fell to Day 3 of the NFL Draft, but he offers more receiving upside than Nate Boerkircher (who was drafted ahead of him). It may be worth betting on Koziol to become the pass catcher if Brenton Strange becomes a free agent after this season.

Anthony spent his career at Wisconsin and posted back-to-back 300-yard receiving seasons. He shows good short-area quickness and catch technique. Anthony went undrafted and will battle to make the Falcons.
Anthony Smith went 64-1053-7 at ECU this past season showcasing good footwork and hands. He could make the Cowboys roster after being drafted late.

Morton spent five years in college at Texas Tech. He helped carry the offense to a playoff berth, but Morton is a bit of a one-trick pony. He has a strong arm and not much else. Morton will have an uphill battle to make an impact on the Patriots' roster.

Hollins went 46-550-2 in 2025 -- his best season since 2022. He did end the season on IR, but Hollins should stick on the Patriots roster. He could be useful in spot starts, but at age-33, he is nearing the end of his career.
Brown only went 20-227-1 on the Jaguars last season and was hurt by the midseason trade for Jakobi Meyers. Brown returned to Washington in free agency, but he'll likely settle into a reserve role.
Walker has become a special-teams player for the Ravens, mixing in the occasional route. If they address WR at all, Walker may be further down the depth chart in 2026.
Sturdivant showed some early promise in college at Cal, but struggled to make a major impact at Florida in 2025. Sturdivant went undrafted and will have an uphill battle to make the Packers' roster.
Tinsley got some work in 2025 when Ja'Marr Chase or Tee Higgins missed time. The Bengals will likely upgrade the back-end of the WRs, but Tinsley can be rosterable in deep leagues until that happens.

Fagnano started his college career at Maine before transferring to UConn. He has good size at 6'4, 225 pounds and can scramble when given the chance. Fagnano improved every season but likely sits as an NFL backup at best. Fagnano went undrafted

Royer transferred from Ohio State to Cincinnati. He went 29-416-4 with QB Brendan Sorsby, a potential Round 1 pick in 2027. A soft hands in-line TE at 6'5, 250 pounds, Royer will make his NFL bones as a blocker. He could be the occasional end zone target as well, but don't expect huge fantasy numbers. He should settle as the No. 2 TE next to Harold Fannin. That would make Royer mainly a blocker.

Bentley earned the starting job at Utah, going 48-620-6 this past season. A solid blocker with soft hands, he lacks starting TE upside but could contribute as a No. 2 TE. He will be a blocking reserve at best for the Broncos.

After four years at FIU, Owens finally got his crack as the starting RB and thrived. He went 213-1334-11 on the ground this year, averaging 6.26 yards per carry. He doesn't offer much in the receiving game, but a thick built and smooth movements makes him an intriguing developmental RB.
Aguilar went undrafted after being a very late declare due to NCAA eligibility issues.

After a bit of a 2025 revival (26-335-1 in nine games), Dulcich signed a fresh one-year deal in Miami. The Fins figure to roll out a low-volume passing game, but Dulcich could be a starting TE, depending on their other offseason additions.

Bullock had a five-year career with South Alabama, improving his output every season. He ended his final year going 218-1045-14. A power gap runner with good vision and balance, he has the skillset to be an NFL reserve.

Hemby flashed playing for Maryland in his first four seasons, but he finally succeeded as the lead back for Indiana. Hemby hit 1,000 rushing yards for the first time in his career. He plays with a low pad level at 6'0 tall and sees cutback lanes cleanly. Hemby was also used as a receiver at Maryland and has more ability there than he showed in 2025 (17 catches). Hemby went undrafted.
In 2025, Fant flashed a bit but played behind Mike Gesicki when Gesicki was healthy. Then in March, he signed a two-year deal with New Orleans, where he'll compete for snaps alongside Juwan Johnson.
Dortch stepped up at times when injuries hit the Cardinals' WRs in 2025, but he ended the season on IR. He joined former Arizona OC Drew Petzing in Detroit and will remain a depth piece.
Wilson flashed early, but struggled to even be active for most of the season. With the Steelers expected to address WR this offseason, Wilson could be on the roster bubble in 2026.
Metchie flashed in 2025 after injuries struck the Jets' WR corps. He finished at 33-274-2. He should stick with the Jets roster as a reserve and could be useful if injuries hit again.

Don't let Montgomery's school, John Carroll, fool you. He can play. The former LSU basketball walk-on never played football in high school. He ended up at John Carroll and became one of the best Division III players in the country. With electric quick-twitch movement, he is extremely difficult to cover and was a star during the Senior Bowl practices. He went undrafted and will have an uphill battle to make the Titans.

Donaldson started his college career as a FB, but his between-the-tackles ability grew to a sizable role at West Virginia. He had two straight seasons with 730+ yards and 11 rushing TDs. The transfer to Ohio State didn't help much as he fell behind freshman RB Bo Jackson. Donaldson went undrafted.
Robinson joined the 49ers in 2025 and played more toward the end of the season, given injuries in front of him. He'll retain a reserve role in 2026.

Haynes made the switch from WR to RB after his freshman year at Georgia Tech and immediately made an impact. He ran for 1,059 yards and 7 TDs as a sophomore. He showcased his receiving in his sophomore season with 944 rushing yards and 166 receiving yards along with 12 total TDs. He took a step back in 2025, splitting carries in the RB room and only rushing for 531 yards. Haynes went undrafted.

Kacmarek played second fiddle to Max Klare at Ohio State, but he was just as reliable a target. A blocker first with long arms at 6'6, Kacmarek does have soft hands and is dependable underneath. Kacmarek got Day 2 draft capital, but he will be exclusively a blocking TE. The playing time may be good, but he won't put up consistent fantasy numbers.

Kolar was a productive, multi-year starter at Iowa State. But he played behind Isaiah Likely and Mark Andrews for four seasons in Baltimore, catching only 30 passes. He signed a three-year deal with the Chargers in free agency, one that includes $17 million in guaranteed money. Still, he figures to contribute heavily in the blocking game for OC Mike McDaniel.
Mingo spent much of 2025 injured and was not able to crack the Cowboys' lineup. He ended the year with one catch and is unlikely to make a fantasy impact in the future.

Hudson broke out in his final year at SMU, going 61-766-6. A slot WR who explodes off the line, Hudson's sure hands make him a threat for short-yardage catches. He offers little upside, but he could make the Cowboys' roster.

Hilton brings blazing speed at 6'0, 190 pounds. He is a pure upside projection, though, with little college production. His best season -- 2023 -- turned up only 13 catches as a deep threat for LSU. Hilton went undrafted and will have an uphill battle to make the Commanders' roster.

Walthall flashed at Hawaii but really broke out at Incarnate Word. He had a 220-yard receiving game and dominanted against weaker competition. Walthall went undrafted and will have an uphill battle to make the Texans' roster.

Bellinger posted a quiet 19-286-2 line in 2025. But he signed a notable three-year deal worth $14 million guaranteed in March, reuniting him with his former HC, Brian Daboll. Bellinger will push second-year TE Gunnar Helm for playing time.
Davis is a special teamer who gets the occasional touch, but he will be unlikely to make a fantasy impact in 2026 and beyond.
Evans had a few shining moments in 2025. However, the lack of a passing game for the Panthers and a TE room full of second-tier options doesn't give Evans much space to emerge.

Tipton managed 14 catches for 99 yards as a rookie UDFA in 2024, and he got some playing time in 2025. It's unlikely we'll see much from Tipton moving forward, but he could stick on the Saints' roster.

Brinson started his career at Miami before transferring to SMU. He stuck around and finally broke out in 2025, going 43-638-3. He has good size and athleticism, but the lack of experience leaves Brinson as a Day 3 pick.
Means flashed a bit in 2024, but he didn't get much playing time in 2025. He is unlikely to make a future impact.

Hibner's size and quick feet make him a great blocking TE and a reliable red zone option. He went 31-436-4 at SMU last year and worked more downfield as the season went on. Hibner could make the Ravens' roster as their No. 2 TE, but he will mostly be an in-line blocker.
Hyatt played poorly again in 2025 and is a cut candidate in 2026.
Higbee signed a new two-year deal with the Rams in March. A crowded TE depth chart continues to make him an unlikely fantasy factor.

Sharp was a high school QB who transitioned to TE at SE Louisiana. He transferred up to Oklahoma, then LSU, as he improved at the position. Sharp is still raw, going for 24-252-2 this past season. Sharp will have to battle to make the Buccaneers' roster.

Kanak only has one year of TE experience under his belt. He started his career as an LB before transitioning to TE in 2025. He went 44-533-0 despite the unfamiliarity with the position. Kanak will likely be a practice squad candidate for the Titans.
The Pats took Polk in Round 2, then moved on from him after just one season. Even at just 24, he's quickly approaching "bust" status.
Osborn signed with the Titans and will likely be a reserve WR for them in 2026.
Jayden Daniels supplies a long-term QB, and the WR room looks thin on talent. Brooks is simply a long shot after failing to record a catch as a rookie.

Ryan went 45-620-3 in his final season at BYU. He flashes good power when blocking with some red zone targets that could make him intriguing. He will likely compete with fellow rookie TE Joe Royer to make the Browns' roster.
Waller returned from retirement in 2025 and offered the Dolphins a red zone target. Turning 34 early next season, there's a chance he heads back to retirement.
Tonges found success when George Kittle was injured, but there's no value here when the veteran is healthy.
Cowing wasn't able to make an impact in 2025, landing on IR with a hamstring injury.

Knox overachieved in 2025, given Dalton Kincaid's injury struggles. The result: 36 catches, 417 yards, and 4 TDs, giving him his best fantasy output since 2022. He can be usable when Kincaid is on the sideline, but Knox won't be a consistent fantasy presence. In March, he agreed to a revised contract to remain with the Bills.
Musgrave hasn't emerged as a legit fantasy threat, even after Tucker Kraft's ACL tear last season. Musgrave will return to compete for a backup role in 2026.
Lovett recorded no catches as a rookie and had one kick return. He profiles as a special teamer if he makes the roster next year.

Cuevas found his largest role on Alabama's offense in 2025, going for 37-411-4. He figures to be a primary blocking TE at 256 pounds, but Cuevas does offer some red zone upside with his contested catching ability. Cuevas flashed a bit of receiving upside, but he will have to compete with fellow rookie Matthew Hibner for playing time behind Mark Andrews.
Gould is still a special teams player with speed who makes little fantasy impact.

Wallace transferred from Penn State to Ole Miss, immediately becoming their top WR. He went 61-934-4 this season as the main target for QB Trinidad Chambliss. A sharp route runner who finds ways to get open, especially against zone coverage, he has intriguing upside. Wallace went undrafted.

Anderson broke out in 2024, posting 61-884-5 on LSU. He dealt with multiple injuries in 2025 that caused Anderson to miss games and barely play in others. He will need to clear the medical check after elbow, toe, and hip ailments. Anderson went undrafted.
McCulley dealt with injuries in 2024 and was pushed out of Indiana. He transferred to Michigan, where he tallied 39-588-3 on a run-first offense. He is a big X WR. McCulley went undrafted.
Roberts had two straight years at BYU of 770+ receiving yards and 4+ TDs. A big-bodied, long-armed X WR, Roberts wins with physicality but won't excite with athleticism. Roberts went undrafted

Nowakowski was overshadowed by the WRs at Indiana, but he was a reliable target for Fernando Mendoza. His 32-387-2 season in 2025 showcased strong blocking and great stick routes against zone coverage. Nowakowski will profile as the third TE on the Steelers

Rogers showcased his athleticism last year for TCU. At 6'4, 250 pounds, he brings intriguing upside but never put everything together for consistent playing time. He went 34-319-2 in 2025. Rogers will likely be a late-round pick as a developmental receiving TE.
Tremble re-signed with the Panthers in 2025, but he posted another quiet season in the fall (27-249-2). There's not much fantasy value to chase here.

Lacy broke out in 2023 at South Alabama, going 91-1316-7. He is a small deep threat WR who was underutilized after transferring to Louisville. Lacy's highlights are incredible, but they are few and far between. Lacy went undrafted.

Sinnott got an opportunity when Zach Ertz went down with an injury in 2025, but he wasn't able to do much with the playing time. Sinnott enters the key third year, but there is no indication that he will carry a sizable role. That's especially true after Washington added Chig Okonkwo on a free agent deal.

Stover appeared in only nine games this past season due to a broken foot. He suffered a partially torn MCL and PCL damage in the postseason but avoided surgery. He'll return as a depth piece behind Dalton Schultz.

Thomas flashed at Texas A&M in 2024, going for 39-574-8. He transferred to Georgia, but didn't find a starting role. He didn't even play in the SEC Championship game and failed to record a catch in their lone playoff game. Thomas went undrafted.
Pauling failed to get a Combine invite, but his speed and athleticism could make him an intriguing UDFA. He could make the end of the 49ers roster.
Hawes is a blocking TE, but the Bills still utilized him at times in the red zone. He doesn't offer much upside as long as Dalton Kincaid is on the roster.

Traore peaked in 2022 for Arkansas State going 50-655-4, but he was a reliable receiving option for Mississippi State the last two years. Traore will be an International Player Pathway player and not count against the Dolphins' roster. He will likely take time to develop.
Reiman dealt with injuries for most of last season and is stuck behind Trey McBride. Reiman has two years left on his rookie deal.
Briningstool arrived as a 2025 UDFA, but a hamstring injury erased his rookie season. He'll return to compete for a depth role in 2026.
Allen posted a career-best 24-208-3 line in 2025. But with Terrance Ferguson on the ascent, Allen is unlikely to become a fantasy factor in L.A.
Schoonmaker sits behind Jake Ferguson on the depth chart and doesn't have a path to playing time for the foreseeable future.
Conklin saw some work as a starter in 2025, but he was surpassed by Oronde Gadsden by midseason. The veteran doesn't offer much upside after signing in Detroit.
Ruckert signed a new deal to stay with the Jets in December. He projects as the No. 2 TE behind second-year man Mason Taylor.

Trigg was a highly recruited TE who started at USC. He transferred to Ole Miss and did very little before finally finding a good home at Baylor. His output: 50 catches, 694 yards, and 6 scores. Trigg's athleticism allows him to do damage after the catch at 6'4, 240 pounds. Trigg went undrafted due to character and effort concerns, but there is an opening to make the Cowboys' roster.

Maryland is a receiving first TE who had consistent production over his four years at SMU. Though he won't test very well, Maryland could make a fantasy impact if he receives playing time. He went undrafted, but could make the Packers' team as the No. 3 TE.

Gyllenborg flashed as a receiving weapon for Wyoming in 2024, going 30-425-3. He dealt with injuries in 2025, taking a step back, but Gyllenborg would be a receiving TE who could emerge with opportunity. He went undrafted, but the Chiefs are a good situation for Gyllenborg to make the roster.

Dippre transferred out of Maryland ahead of the 2023 season. He chose Alabama over Ohio State in the transfer portal, although big-time production never followed (32 catches, 441 yards, and 0 TDs over the past two seasons). Dippre ultimately checks boxes for size and play strength, although he went undrafted in 2025. He was held without a catch as a rookie.
Culp caught a TD last season, but he played only 56 snaps as a rookie. There's not much of a ceiling here, especially after the Bucs re-signed Cade Otton.
Hill will turn 36 before next season and hits the offseason as a free agent. There's a chance he retires, but there's no fantasy value to find here, regardless.

McRee spent his entire five-year career at USC and peaked in 2025, going for 30-450-4. McRee is a solid blocker and a soft hands catcher who was a reliable third-down option for the Trojans. McRee went undrafted and is unlikely to make the Steelers' roster.
Bredeson likely will play a FB/H-Back role in the NFL which may make him unusable for fantasy. He is a blocker first who catches the occasional pass. He will be a FB on the Vikings.

Wright has some upside as a pass catching TE who went 39-635-5 at Ole Miss this past season. He remains a bit stiff in his route running and can drop passes, but there is some upside. Wright went undrafted.
Dinkins never really broke out for Penn State, but the athletic TE was a seam buster who could catch a long TD. He would be a developmental NFL prospect. Dinkins went undrafted.
Kitselman started his career at Alabama before transferring to Tennessee. He had two solid seasons but brings more blocking prowess than receiving upside. He went undrafted.

Villari started his college career as a QB at Michigan before transferring to Syracuse to play TE. Despite the team's poor QB play, Villari was a mainstay as a possession TE who can play in the slot and be a weapon. He also had 17 rushes for 55 yards and a TD this season. Villari went undrafted.
